text.title: RFC 7807 Error Handling Antipatterns
text.summary: Failure modes when implementing Problem Details error responses.

antipattern.description: Interpolating exception messages, stack traces, SQL text, or internal paths into the detail field for 5xx errors.
antipattern.reason: Exposes implementation details, internal hostnames, and sometimes credentials to external clients. Use a static detail string and provide traceId for internal lookup.

antipattern.description: Returning Content-Type: application/json instead of application/problem+json.
antipattern.reason: Clients that detect the +json media type suffix (e.g., Spring ProblemDetail) fall back to generic parsing. RFC 7807 mandates the problem+json type; the methodolgy's consistency guarantee depends on it.

antipattern.description: HTTP status code on the response does not match the status field in the body.
antipattern.reason: RFC 7807 requires both to match. Mismatch appears when wrapping downstream errors: the HTTP envelope says 502 while the body says 400, confusing clients and monitoring tools.

antipattern.description: Using errors[] for non-validation error types (401, 403, 404, 429).
antipattern.reason: errors[] is a field-level extension for validation failures. Single-cause errors carry all necessary information in detail. Adding an empty errors[] array confuses clients expecting the spec shape.

antipattern.description: type field contains a non-URI string such as "validation" or "not_found".
antipattern.reason: RFC 7807 requires type to be a URI. Non-URI values are technically non-conforming. More importantly, they cannot link to documentation pages that explain the error, recovery steps, and errors[] shape.

antipattern.description: Skipping traceId on successful error handling (only populating it on 500).
antipattern.reason: Tracing 400-class errors is equally important for debugging client-integration issues. Always populate traceId from W3C traceparent or generate a UUID.

antipattern.description: errors[].code contains a translated or localized string.
antipattern.reason: code is machine-readable and must be stable. Translate errors[].message for users, but keep code as an invariant slug (email_invalid, not "Email invalide").
